goal: Implement P1.1: make the orchestrate loop's pause button actually halt execution. The pause_requested flag exists on OrchestrateRun and the UI endpoint flips it, but the executor loop never checks it — users click Pause yet the run keeps consuming tasks and budget.

tasks.name: Scaffold minimal platform workspace
tasks.description: Create the project directory structure and install dependencies needed to run the FastAPI platform service.
tasks.instruction: Set up the workspace: create platform/ package with app/__init__.py, install fastapi, uvicorn, sqlalchemy, alembic, psycopg2-binary, pytest, httpx into platform/requirements.txt. Create platform/app/main.py with a FastAPI app and health endpoint GET /health â†’ 200 {status: ok}. Create alembic.ini and alembic/env.py wired to DATABASE_URL env var. Create tests/ directory with conftest.py. Verify the test file can be collected.

acceptance_criteria.text: pytest --collect-only succeeds without import errors on platform/tests/

tasks.name: Add OrchestrateRun model and pause fields
tasks.description: Define the OrchestrateRun SQLAlchemy model with pause_requested and paused_at fields, run the migration.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/models.py create OrchestrateRun with columns: id (UUID PK), status (String, default 'RUNNING'), pause_requested (Boolean, default False, non-nullable), paused_at (DateTime, nullable), created_at, updated_at. Generate and run alembic migration. Expose a get_db() dependency in platform/app/database.py using TEST_DATABASE_URL env var for tests.

acceptance_criteria.text: OrchestrateRun row can be created with pause_requested=False and queried back

tasks.name: Add _check_pause() cooperative gate in executor loop
tasks.description: Implement the pause check inside the orchestration executor so the loop halts when pause_requested is set.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/executor.py implement _check_pause(db, run_id) -> bool: re-fetches OrchestrateRun from DB, returns True if pause_requested=True, else False. Implement run_orchestration(run_id, db): loops, calls _check_pause â€” if True sets run.status='PAUSED', run.paused_at=utcnow(), commits, returns. This cooperative gate is the core fix: the loop now checks the flag on each iteration.

acceptance_criteria.text: _check_pause returns True when pause_requested=True in DB (fresh read, detects concurrent update)
run_orchestration sets status=PAUSED and paused_at when pause flag is set

produces.function: _check_pause(db, run_id) â†’ True when pause_requested=True (fresh DB read)

tasks.name: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/pause endpoint
tasks.description: Expose an API endpoint that sets pause_requested=True on the target run.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/routers/orchestrate.py add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/pause: fetch OrchestrateRun by id (404 if not found, 409 if status is terminal DONE/FAILED/CANCELLED), set pause_requested=True, commit, return 200 {id, pause_requested: true, status}. Wire the router into platform/app/main.py.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /pause returns 200 and sets pause_requested=True for RUNNING run
POST /pause returns 404 for non-existent run id

tasks.name: Add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume endpoint
tasks.description: Expose an API endpoint that clears pause_requested and paused_at, allowing the run to continue.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/routers/orchestrate.py add POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume: fetch run (404 if not found, 409 if not in PAUSED status), set pause_requested=False, paused_at=None, status='RUNNING', commit, return 200 {id, pause_requested: false, status: RUNNING}.

acceptance_criteria.text: POST /resume clears pause_requested and paused_at for PAUSED run, returns status=RUNNING
POST /resume returns 409 when run is not in PAUSED state

produces.endpoint: POST /orchestrate-runs/{id}/resume â†’ 200 {id, pause_requested: false, status: RUNNING}

tasks.name: Expose pause fields in run status endpoint
tasks.description: Return pause_requested and paused_at in GET /orchestrate-runs/{id}/status so the UI can reflect pause state.
tasks.instruction: In platform/app/routers/orchestrate.py add GET /orchestrate-runs/{id}/status: fetch run (404 if not found), return 200 {id, status, pause_requested, paused_at}. Add Pydantic response schema RunStatusResponse with these fields. This closes the UI feedback loop.

acceptance_criteria.text: GET /status returns pause_requested=True and non-null paused_at for a paused run
GET /status returns pause_requested=False and paused_at=null for a running run
